sourceMovie.title: Drunken Master
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Drunken Master (1978) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Legend of Drunken Master
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Drunken Master clicked for you, The Legend of Drunken Master (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Drunken Master?
faq.answer: Try The Legend of Drunken Master, No Retreat, No Surrender, Snake in the Eagle's Shadow—each shares DNA with Drunken Master while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Drunken Master?
faq.answer: Drunken Master (1978) is best described as Action, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Drunken Master part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Drunken Master Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
